summary: Retire install's CI-workflow projection — install places only the hook + managed header lines; the CI gate is a documented user-authored job, not a generated file

per.section: Decision: `install` is two placements, one mechanism

edit.description: Remove the CI-workflow projection: the `CI_WORKFLOW` string const (install.rs:63-84) and `CI_JOB` label (install.rs:86), the `.github/workflows/temper.yml` `drift::place` call site (install.rs:299-303), and the `ci` arm of the `gate_installed` missing/drifted tally (install.rs:228,236,241,252). The re-cut rejects an install-managed CI workflow file outright (rejected (b)); `install` places exactly the SessionStart hook and the managed header lines (schema modeline + managed-by note). Leave the hook, the advisory guard (open fork `(guard-posture)`), the modelines, and the notes untouched — this entry only excises CI.

Drop the CI-job assertions and snapshot rows (the only test references to `temper.yml`/`ci job`/`workflows` live here): assert `install` writes no `.github/workflows/temper.yml`, and that `gate_installed` neither expects nor reports a CI-job placement.

tests.asserts: `temper install` produces no `.github/workflows/temper.yml`; `gate_installed` reports no missing/drifted CI job; the hook, guard, modelines, and notes still place as before.

acceptance: `temper install` writes no CI workflow file; `gate_installed` no longer tallies a CI job; install's placements are the SessionStart hook, the advisory guard, the schema modelines, and the managed-by notes; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Resolves the inbox CI bug (installed workflow runs the retired `temper import . --into .temper`, exit 127) at its root: 50-distribution rejects an install-managed CI workflow — CI is a documented two-line user-authored job. First landed slice of the six-noun demolition. Accepted debt (human): temper's own now-unmanaged `.github/workflows/temper.yml` stays broken until replaced with that hand-authored job (a chore(harness)/repo-infra fix, not a build tick).

summary: Drift's lock fingerprint map enumerates the actually-embedded built-in kinds, not the stale `kind::BUILTIN_KINDS` = [skill, rule] const — so memory (and future) kind rows are seen

per.section: Decision: kinds are declared data over generic extraction, never engine code

edit.description: The two loops at drift.rs:635 (build the source-path -> emit_hash map) and drift.rs:672 (write reconciled fingerprints back) iterate `kind::BUILTIN_KINDS`, a hardcoded ["skill","rule"] (kind.rs:30). A `memory` member's [[memory]] lock rows are therefore never fingerprinted, so a hand-edited memory projection is invisible to drift. Enumerate the bare names of the actually-embedded kinds — derive them from the build.rs-generated `builtin_kind::BUILTIN_KINDS` (`<provider>.<name>` table) — instead of the stale const. Add a `#[cfg(test)]` case exercising a memory lock row. Leave `kind::BUILTIN_KINDS` in place (compose.rs:3154 still reads it) — redirect drift only, keeping the blast radius to drift.rs alone.

tests.asserts: the fingerprint map built from a lock carrying [[memory]] rows includes those rows; the update loop rewrites a memory row's emit_hash; skill/rule behavior is unchanged.

acceptance: drift's load + update lock loops cover every embedded built-in kind (memory included), not just skill/rule; a hand-edited memory projection is detected as drift; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Ceremony-review bug #2. The manifest-era per-kind-table lock reshapes under the eventual demolition (disk-vs-lock, declaration rows), but the bug is live now and the fix is local. Disjoint from the other pending entries (drift.rs only; `builtin_kind::BUILTIN_KINDS` is read, not edited).

summary: Read's `bound_package` resolves each kind's real floor instead of defaulting every non-rule kind to the skill floor — so a memory member isn't mis-narrated as skill-bound

edit.description: `bound_package` (read.rs:147-156) hardcodes `match kind { "rule" => RULE_PACKAGE, _ => SKILL_PACKAGE }`, so every non-rule/non-skill kind (memory, and any future built-in) is narrated as bound to `skill.anthropic`. Resolve the kind's qualified identity and use `builtin::floor_package` (builtin.rs:82, the `QUALIFIED_FLOOR_BINDINGS` lookup) to name the real floor, keeping the author-layer explicit binding as the override and falling back only when a kind genuinely has no floor. builtin.rs is read, not edited.
Assert a memory member's read output names its own floor (`memory.anthropic` / `memory.agents-md`), not `skill.anthropic`; keep the existing skill/rule narration assertions green.

tests.asserts: read narrates a memory member as bound to its `memory.*` floor; skill/rule members still narrate their own floors; an explicit author-layer binding still overrides.

acceptance: read's `bound_package` names the correct floor for every embedded kind; a memory member is narrated as bound to its `memory.*` floor, never `skill.anthropic`; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Ceremony-review bug #1. Read-only correctness; `builtin::floor_package` already maps the qualified identity, so the fix routes `bound_package` through it. Disjoint from the other pending entries (read.rs + tests/read_verbs.rs). Survives the eventual read-verb consolidation to a single `explain`.

summary: Ship acquisition channels — npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install — auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org + @temper scope, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone release assets), GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI check job (which under the re-cut is a documented user-authored job, not a generated file).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, 50-distribution). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than clobbering it. Scoped `@temper` names per the resolved (project-name) fork. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (the SDK authoring-face manifest for `temper` + `@temper/claude-code`).

acceptance: A stranger acquires the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infra, mostly outside `src/`; cargo gates can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human creds. Re-cut 2026-07-04: acquisition intent now lives in 50-distribution 'Three channels' (npm `temper` + `@temper/claude-code`; the binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; the plugin/bundle). Root `package.json` is the private flume manifest (not sdk/package.json) — reconcile or split. Parked on human release creds.
